task_completion.description: Open the target image in the GIMP application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open and displays the target image (either a photograph or a white canvas with a blue rectangle and text).
The 'Image' dropdown menu in the top menu bar of GIMP is clicked and expanded.
The 'Crop to Content' or 'Autocrop Image' option is highlighted or clicked within the Image dropdown menu.
The image displayed in the GIMP workspace is visibly smaller, with the outer borders or whitespace removed, while the core visual content remains intact.

task_completion.description: Save or export the modified image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'File' menu shows 'Overwrite [filename]' or 'Export As' being selected, or an export/save confirmation dialog is visible and being confirmed.

integrity.description: Agent used the GIMP GUI rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIMP graphical interface for cropping and saving, rather than a terminal window executing ImageMagick or other CLI commands.

integrity.description: Agent used an automatic crop function rather than manual cropping
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the 'Image -> Crop to Content' or 'Autocrop Image' menu option. The manual Crop Tool (knife icon) and manual bounding box adjustments should NOT be used.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely saved the modified image
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent completes the save/export dialogs or uses the overwrite function, rather than just modifying the image in the workspace and closing the application without saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP to automatically remove borders/whitespace from an image (either a 600x400 Unsplash photo or an 800x600 synthetic image with a blue rectangle). The agent must use an automatic feature like 'Image -> Crop to Content' or 'Autocrop Image', strictly avoiding manual cropping tools. The final image must be saved/exported. Successful programmatic verification requires dimension reduction (>=10px or >=5%), content preservation (SSIM >= 0.90), and improved edge density (>= 1.2x).
